subject: English / First Flight
overall_learning_objectives: Understand the imagery and movement described in the poem
Recognize personification and its effect in poetry
Comprehend the theme of transience and fleeting moments in nature
Develop basic comprehension, summarization, and observation skills
Encourage creative expression through discussion and writing

lesson_plan.title: Introduction to the Poem and Poet
teaching_strategy: Introduce Carl Sandburg and his style using simple examples
Ask students to notice fog or mist in real life and describe its movement
student_activities: Predict what the poem might describe based on the title
Share personal experiences of observing fog or mist
assessment: Oral Q&A on poet and poem title
Check students’ predictions for engagement and imagination

lesson_plan.title: Reading and Comprehension
topics_covered: Explain difficult words: harbour, haunches, silent
Visualize and discuss the fog’s movement
teaching_strategy: Read line by line and explain imagery and personification
Encourage students to picture fog moving like a cat over the city
student_activities: Underline phrases showing movement and description of fog
Write a short summary of what the fog does in 2–3 sentences
assessment: Ask students to explain how the fog moves and why it is compared to a cat
Check written summaries for comprehension and clarity

lesson_plan.title: Theme, Mood, and Creative Reflection
topics_covered: Discuss the theme of transience and fleeting moments
Explore mood and atmosphere created by the poem
Connect imagery to personal observation of nature
teaching_strategy: Explain how the poem’s brevity emphasizes the fleeting nature of fog
Discuss how simple words can create a calm and reflective mood
student_activities: Describe a scene of fog in their own words
Write 3–4 lines imagining fog moving over their town or school
Draw a small illustration of fog over a landscape
assessment: Class discussion on theme, mood, and imagery

teaching_aids: Images of fog over cities, harbours, and landscapes
Charts highlighting personification and key imagery
Flashcards with vocabulary and difficult words
evaluation_methods: Discussion on theme, mood, and literary devices
teacher_tip: Keep explanations simple and engaging. Emphasize the quiet and graceful movement of fog using personification. Encourage students to observe natural phenomena like fog and relate them to the poem for better understanding.
